title: Module 1: Introduction to ML Quiz
description: Test your understanding of machine learning fundamentals

questions.question: What is the main difference between traditional programming and machine learning?
options: ML learns patterns from data while traditional programming uses explicit rules
ML is faster than traditional programming
Traditional programming can't handle large datasets
questions.explanation: In traditional programming, developers write explicit rules. In ML, algorithms learn patterns from data automatically.

questions.question: Which type of machine learning uses labeled training data?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning requires labeled data where the correct answers (labels) are provided during training.

questions.question: What are the three core components of every ML system?
questions.explanation: Every ML system needs: (1) Data to learn from, (2) A model to represent patterns, and (3) An algorithm to optimize the model.

questions.question: Which ML task would you use to predict house prices based on features like size and location?
questions.explanation: Regression is used for predicting continuous numerical values like prices, temperatures, or ages.

questions.question: What type of learning would be best for grouping customers by purchasing behavior without predefined categories?
questions.explanation: Unsupervised clustering discovers natural groupings in data without requiring labeled categories.

questions.question: In reinforcement learning, what does an agent receive after taking an action?
questions.explanation: Reinforcement learning agents receive rewards (positive) or penalties (negative) as feedback to learn optimal behavior.

questions.question: Which scenario is best suited for machine learning over traditional programming?
options: Calculating the sum of two numbers
Converting temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit
questions.explanation: Face recognition involves complex patterns that are too difficult to define with explicit rules, making ML ideal.

questions.question: What is an example of anomaly detection?
questions.explanation: Anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns that deviate from normal behavior, like fraudulent transactions.
